Following the onsite SBR in Austin with Akamai leadership, Adobe and Akamai are aligned on next‑phase priorities and opportunities to further drive value. The session highlighted measurable outcomes delivered through Ultimate Success and reinforced alignment with Akamai’s FY26 focus areas, including driving growth across high‑priority cloud and security segments, increasing brand awareness and authority, and improving visibility across the customer journey.
As a follow‑up to the SBR, the teams aligned on several next steps, including initiating a GenStudio for Performance Marketing discovery focused on AI‑driven content acceleration, refining governance and engagement cadence, exploring Commerce and Marketo paths for Akamai CDN, and conducting deeper discovery around CJA B2B to assess current‑state gaps and future‑state analytics needs.
Akamai also shared clear feedback regarding Marketo, noting that they have not seen meaningful innovation or process improvement from the solution in recent years. They emphasized the importance of seeing tangible progress and evolution ahead of their FY27 renewal. In response, the account team will ensure tighter alignment between Akamai’s Marketo stakeholders and Adobe Product to review current use cases, validate roadmap alignment, and identify opportunities to demonstrate continued value and innovation.

4/15/25 (KR): Overall partnership with Christie's for Analytics and Target remains strong. However, RTCDP Connections has moved to Red given it is due for renewal next month, and Christie's and is struggling to justify keeping the tool. They originally purchased Connections in 2023 in preparation for third-party cookie deprecation, but with Google rescinding their EOL, Christie’s is lacking in finding deeper use cases relevant to their business (luxury retail auctions). The original product owner in the US recently left Christie’s, and the new product owner in the UK does not have any information on what the tool does, how to login and access it, or where their implementation stands. Their usage report is extremely low, so we have reason to believe it is not fully implemented across their larger API connectors (Meta, Google, etc.). but have not been able to track down a way internally of where we can find this information.

This initiative is not only a crucial use case but also a vital channel for future personalization efforts, as they plan to increasingly leverage Adobe to display more offers on the app homescreen. A recent notable achievement was the Chipotle leadership team’s endorsement, stating, “Adobe Analytics is more dependable than ever” and referring to it as “our golden record"
